description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a camouflage paint scheme, featuring two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked one above the other and connected by struts and wires. The fuselage is streamlined and tapers towards the tail, with a single propeller engine mounted at the nose. The cockpit is positioned just behind the upper wing and appears to be open, with a small windshield for pilot protection. The tail section includes a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, with a tailwheel landing gear configuration. The roundels and markings on the wings and fuselage suggest a military origin, likely from the World War II era. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tailwheel, all fixed and non-retractable.
description: A vintage military biplane with camouflage paint, dual stacked wings, a single nose-mounted propeller, and an open cockpit behind the upper wing.
description_view_0: Side view showing the camouflage pattern, open cockpit, and fixed landing gear with a clear view of the propeller and tailwheel.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the dual stacked wings, roundels on the wingtips, and the single propeller at the nose.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the fuselage markings, cockpit, and the tail section with its stabilizers and tailwheel.
description_view_3: Head-on view emphasizing the biplane's wing structure, propeller, and the symmetry of the landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and connected by struts and wires; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a single tailwheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located just behind the upper wing, likely single-seat with a small windshield.
